_purpose: v0.2.1 corpus expansion: bug regressions + multi-family compounds + controls expansion.
_instructions: Splice the 'additions' dict into the existing 'families' dict. For buckets that already exist (us_visa, controls), append to the existing arrays. For new buckets (recent_contract_vessel_experience, multi), the splicer creates them. The splicer MUST skip any underscore-prefixed keys inside 'additions' as defensive metadata-safety. See HANDOFF_v0.2.1_corpus_expansion.md.
_schema_note: For rows with expected_primary_family='multi', the top-level keys of expected_constraint MUST be family IDs (age_range, rank_match, certificate_requirement, etc.), NOT hard_constraints schema keys (age_years, rank, certifications). The scorer's _expected_families() reads these keys literally and looks them up against the catalog via GOLD_TO_PLAN_FAMILY. Using schema keys causes phantom-family attribution. The committed v0.2 row 'valid US visa and passport' follows this convention.
_visa_accepted_types_note: USA accepted_types canonical list per _visa_type_definitions() (group='usa') is exactly: ['C1/D (USA)', 'B1/B2 (USA)', 'C1 (USA)', 'D (USA)', 'US Visa (USA)']. Note the '(USA)' suffix on every entry. Verified via the pre-check snippet in HANDOFF §pre-implementation-checklist step 3. Class shorthands like 'H-1B', 'L-1', 'F-1', 'O-1' are cue regex aliases (used by _SUPPORTED_VISA_CONTEXT_CUES for anchoring) but are NOT in the canonical accepted_types list.

us_visa.note: REGRESSION row for PR #23 bug fix in shadow_llm_provider.py::_visa_accepted_types_for_group. Pre-fix, generic 'US Visa (USA)' with no specific class collapsed accepted_types to ['US Visa (USA)'] placeholder. Post-fix, accepted_types must be the full canonical USA class list. RE-VERIFY accepted_types against live _visa_type_definitions() if the catalog has been updated since 2026-06-10.
REGRESSION row for PR #23 accepted-types fix. Minimal-form generic USA visa prompt — exactly the path where the helper backfills accepted_types from the catalog.

us_visa.prompt: valid visa for USA work assignment
us_visa.note: REGRESSION row for PR #23 accepted-types fix. Narrative phrasing without 'visa' adjacent to 'USA'; cue regex still anchors via 'USA' + 'visa'. accepted_types fallback path.

us_visa.prompt: must have visa for entering the United States
us_visa.note: REGRESSION row for PR #23 accepted-types fix. Formal 'entering the United States' phrasing; cue regex includes 'states' alias. Tests accepted_types backfill on the long-form country reference.

recent_contract_vessel_experience.prompt: 12 months experience on container ship in last 3 contracts
recent_contract_vessel_experience.note: REGRESSION row for PR #23 bug fix in legacy_parser_adapter.py. Pre-fix, legacy emitted non-canonical container vessel as ship_family, which failed query-plan schema validation. Post-fix, ship_family must be canonical container. Uses an explicit recent-contract window so the live legacy extractor emits recent_contract_vessel_experience.

recent_contract_vessel_experience.prompt: container vessel experience in recent 3 contracts
recent_contract_vessel_experience.note: REGRESSION row for PR #23 container canonicalization. The exact 'container vessel' phrasing is the literal substring the buggy adapter emitted as ship_family. Critical post-mortem coverage.

recent_contract_vessel_experience.prompt: must have served on container ships in last 3 contracts
recent_contract_vessel_experience.note: REGRESSION row for PR #23 container canonicalization. Past-tense 'served on' phrasing. ship_family canonicalization must hold regardless of tense.

multi.prompt: Chief Engineer with US visa and passport
multi.note: MULTI: rank_match + us_visa + passport_validity. Legacy catches rank + US visa but misses the passport requirement in this compact compound phrasing.
MULTI: rank_match + stcw_basic. Both parsers fire independently; tests that the LLM emits BOTH constraints together rather than one-at-a-time.

multi.prompt: officers in their 40s with valid passport
multi.note: MULTI: age_range + passport_validity. Decade-span ('in their 40s') is a documented age miss in v0.2; pairing with passport_validity tests whether the LLM can rescue both families simultaneously. Convention: in their 40s -> [40, 49].
MULTI: rank_match (two ratings) + age_range (numeric range). Legacy fires both; tests that the LLM preserves both rather than dropping one in compound parsing.

multi.prompt: Master with H-1B visa and 6 months passport validity
multi.note: MULTI: 3-family compound (rank_match + us_visa + passport_validity). H-1B is a cue alias triggering visa_group=usa but H-1B itself is NOT in the canonical accepted_types list — the helper backfills the standard maritime USA list. accepted_types deliberately omitted from expected_constraint; the eval value-review step confirms what the LLM emits matches the catalog.

multi.prompt: container vessel Chief Officer with valid US visa
multi.note: MULTI: rank_match + us_visa + ship-family (recent_contract_vessel_experience). Doubles as a container canonicalization regression probe (PR #23 fix) in a multi-family context.

multi.prompt: passport valid for 12 months and US visa C1/D
multi.note: MULTI: passport_validity (with months) + us_visa (with specific class). Tests value preservation across compound prompts where each family has structured detail. C1/D is in the canonical accepted_types catalog — narrow to that single canonical value rather than the full USA list.

MULTI: rank_match + age_range + certificate_requirement. Generic 'engineer' rank, strict age bound ('over' -> exclusive +1), advanced cert. NOTE: 'engineer' as a generic alias may or may not be in RANK_ALIAS_TABLE — verify; if missing, this row probes rank_match LLM rescue too.

multi.prompt: younger than 50 with STCW basic and valid passport
multi.note: MULTI: age_range (strict 'younger than' -> max_age N-1) + stcw_basic + passport_validity. Three-family compound with one strict-bound age constraint.

MULTI: ship-family (tanker) + stcw_endorsement. Doubles as ship_family canonicalization coverage for the 'tanker' canonical value AND tests stcw_endorsement family rescue (the 7th promote-candidate family added during v0.2). Convention: 'tanker' alone maps to ship_family='tanker'; 'STCW endorsement' near 'tanker' implies tanker_oil endorsement per catalog.

controls.note: CONTROL for certificate_requirement (family currently has 0 controls). Vague 'any certificate' — shadow LLM must not hallucinate a specific certificates_required list.
CONTROL: cross-family adversarial. Should fire stcw_basic (not certificate_requirement). The LLM must NOT additionally fire certificate_requirement on this prompt.

controls.prompt: any rank acceptable for this slot
controls.note: CONTROL for rank_match (family currently has 0 controls). Anti-constraint on rank.

controls.prompt: open to officers of all grades
controls.note: CONTROL for rank_match. Qualitative 'all grades' is figurative.
CONTROL for stcw_endorsement (the bonus 7th family with 0 controls). Vague — must not pick from canonical_endorsement_values().
CONTROL: cross-family stcw_endorsement vs certificate_requirement. Tests whether the LLM mistakes a single specific course for stcw_endorsement.

controls.prompt: had US visa application rejected last year
controls.note: CONTROL for us_visa: sharper polarity inversion. Rejected != has valid. Current _VISA_POLARITY_INVERSION covers visa-free, no-visa-required, doesn't-need-visa, without-visa, visa-exempt — but NOT 'rejected'.

controls.prompt: needs Canadian visa for crew change
controls.note: CONTROL for us_visa: unsupported country. Canada is outside _SUPPORTED_VISA_CONTEXT_CUES — must not hallucinate as usa.

controls.prompt: Indian tourist visa from last year
controls.note: CONTROL for us_visa: double veto. India is outside supported countries AND tourist class is not maritime.
CONTROL for passport_validity: process-vs-validity distinction. Process state is not a validity assertion.
CONTROL for passport_validity: administrative reference vs validity intent. Tests whether the LLM with anchor veto distinguishes 'passport' as document reference from validity requirement.

CONTROL for age_range: figurative age + rank descriptor. _AGE_FIGURATIVE_PATTERNS covers 'senior captain' so age_range veto should hold. Note: 'captain' may fire rank_match (master) — if it does, this row is effectively informational on rank_match; the age_range veto is what's being tested.
CONTROL for age_range: purely figurative 'mature' with no numeric age — must not fire age_range.
